name: Valle del Almanzora — return from Huércal-Overa
name_original: Valle del Almanzora — vuelta desde Huércal-Overa
description: This difficult 53.8-mile road cycling route from Huércal-Overa explores the scenic Valle del Almanzora, featuring historic sites and mixed t
region_intro: Road cycling around Huércal-Overa offers diverse topography, ranging from flatter sections to rigorous mountain climbs. The region is characterized by the Sierra de las Estancias and Sierra de Filabres mountains, providing challenging ascents. Cyclists can also explore routes through almond fields and olive groves, including sections of the Vía Verde de Huércal-Overa. This varied terrain caters to different skill levels, making it a notable destination for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Huércal-Overa The most popular road cycling route is Valle del Almanzora — return from Huércal-Overa, a 53.8 miles (86.6 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic Almanzora valley with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa, a difficult 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path. This loop offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes, passing by historical points of interest. Local road cyclists also love the Antas Aqueduct – Bends of the Rambla del Serrón loop from Repsol, a 44.5 miles (71.7 km) trail leading through historical aqueduct structures and the natural bends of the Rambla del Serrón, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Huércal-Overa is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de las Estancias, agricultural landscapes with almond and olive groves, and dedicated greenways. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Huércal-Overa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Huércal-Overa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Huércal-Overa?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available in and around Huércal-Overa on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences. The broader region of Almería province, where Huércal-Overa is located, boasts more than 70 cycling routes in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Huércal-Overa?
A: Road cycling around Huércal-Overa features diverse topography. You'll find routes with significant elevation gains through mountain ranges like the Sierra de las Estancias and Sierra de Filabres, as well as flatter sections winding through agricultural landscapes with almond fields and olive groves. The area also includes dedicated greenways, offering varied challenges for all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Huércal-Overa?
A: The Almería province, including Huércal-Overa, is known for its remarkable weather, making it conducive to sports tourism for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying ample sunshine.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: While many routes in the area are rated moderate to difficult, the region does offer options for less experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1565892/road-cycling-routes-around-huercal-overa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt;, for example, follows a disused railway line, providing a relatively flat and scenic path ideal for a more relaxed ride. This route is well-equipped with amenities like rest areas and viewpoints.

Q: Are there challenging routes for advanced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Huércal-Overa is well-suited for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987172353/valle-del-almanzora-return-from-huercal-overa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Almanzora — return from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 86.6 km. More demanding options include the Collado de la Rellena loop with a 570-meter ascent, and longer expeditions such as the Ruta los Velez, which provide substantial climbing challenges through the Sierra de las Estancias and Sierra de Filabres mountains.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Huércal-Overa?
A: Many routes pass by historical and natural points of interest. You might encounter remnants of the old Almanzora railway, including impressive metal and stone bridges. The region also features historical sites such as the 13th-century Castle of Saint Barbara and the Nasrid Watchtower. For natural beauty, look out for the unique landscapes of the Tabernas Desert integrated into some routes, or explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5578174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Calguerín&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dedicated cycling paths or greenways in the area?
A: Yes, a significant highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1565892/road-cycling-routes-around-huercal-overa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Huércal-Overa (Green Way)&lt;/a&gt;. This picturesque route, extending 13 to 17 kilometers, follows the path of a disused railway line, connecting Almajalejo and Las Norias. It's equipped with rest areas, interpretive panels, and viewpoints, making it excellent for cycling.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Huércal-Overa?
A: The road cycling routes in Huércal-Overa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic rides through almond and olive groves, and the well-maintained greenways.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Huércal-Overa?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Huércal-Overa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17248700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38226012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antas Aqueduct – Bends of the Rambla del Serrón loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse landscapes and challenges.

Q: Where can I find parking for road biking trails in Huércal-Overa?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not always listed, Huércal-Overa is a town with general parking facilities. For routes starting directly from the town, you can typically find street parking or public car parks. For routes like the Vía Verde, designated parking areas are often available at key access points along the greenway.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Huércal-Overa, being a popular cycling destination, often has small villages and towns along its routes where you can find local cafes, bars, or shops to refuel. The Vía Verde, for instance, includes rest areas, and the Collado de la Rellena loop mentions a Gastrobar La Estación, indicating that refreshment stops are available on some routes.

Q: Are there any historical castles or ruins accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can cycle past or near several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17248700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical points of interest. Notable castles and ruins in the broader area include the 13th-century Castle of Saint Barbara, the Nasrid Watchtower, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5329205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Marquis of Los Vélez&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route, covering approximately 86.6 kilometers with over 1350 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 4 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance, and considerable elevation gain, this route is generally not recommended for beginners. It's best suited for experienced racebikers comfortable with long distances and sustained climbs.

faqs.question: Where does this racebike route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is named 'Valle del Almanzora — return from Huércal-Overa', indicating a start and return point in or near Huércal-Overa. For parking, look for public parking areas within Huércal-Overa, which is a common starting point for exploring the Almanzora Valley.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for race biking on this route?
faqs.answer: While much of the Almanzora Valley Greenway, which this route may incorporate, features compacted gravel, this specific racebike route is designed for road cycling. You can expect a mix of asphalted sections and potentially some compacted gravel, especially if it utilizes parts of the former railway line. Be prepared for varied surfaces suitable for a racebike capable of handling some mixed terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a beautiful journey through the Almanzora River valley, characterized by historic villages, thermal springs, and protected natural areas. The route also features remnants of its industrial railway heritage, including tunnels and viaducts, adding a unique historical dimension to the landscape. In spring, the valley is particularly vibrant with blooming flowers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights you might encounter include the town of Arboleas, the scenic Bends of the Rambla del Serrón, the historic Cuevas del Almanzora Castle, and the Ruins of the Cuevas de Almanzora Kilns, offering glimpses into the region's past and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Vía Verde del FC. Guadix-Almendricos and the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora, which are part of the region's greenway network, as well as the Sendero de Limaria.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While some sections of the broader Almanzora Valley Greenway may be dog-friendly, this specific racebike route is long and challenging. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are fit enough for the distance and terrain, and always keep them on a leash, especially on roads or near populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Valle del Almanzora. It utilizes public roads and greenway sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is during spring or autumn. Spring offers vibrant blooming flowers and pleasant temperatures, while autumn provides cooler weather ideal for challenging rides. Summers can be very hot in this region, making cycling less comfortable, and winters can be cooler but generally mild.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling the 'return from Huércal-Overa' aspect of this route?
faqs.answer: The Almanzora Valley Greenway generally features a gentle descent when cycling from Baúl towards Arboleas. Since this route is a 'return from Huércal-Overa', be prepared for a more challenging experience, as you'll likely be cycling against this general descent or navigating local roads with significant uphill sections. Plan your energy accordingly and consider starting earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat, especially during warmer months.
